message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_repeat_interleave autograd via sum-of-R backward [bd-5196]

tensor_repeat_interleave used to extract values, repeat each one R
times in plain f64, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf —
silently severing autograd. PyTorch's torch.repeat_interleave IS
differentiable.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward
   grad_input[i] = sum_{j: j / R == i} grad_output[j]
i.e. each input element receives the sum of the R consecutive
output gradients that came from it (the inverse of the consecutive
repeat).

Preserves the byte-size overflow check: checked_mul(in_len, R)
catches element-count overflow but (1, usize::MAX) yields
usize::MAX which fits in usize while the *byte* size overflows
Vec's allocation budget. We add a follow-on
checked_mul(out_len, sizeof::<f64>()) to fail-loud before
tensor_apply_function tries to allocate.

Adds repeat_interleave_propagates_gradient_via_sum_of_repeats
regression test: x=[3, 5], R=4 → out=[3,3,3,3,5,5,5,5], under sum
loss grad=[4, 4] (each input position receives 4 output grads).

Closes frankentorch-5196. ft-api lib tests: 1165 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
